frozen_bugs._read_me: Cells listed under a state='frozen' entry are WRONG ON PURPOSE. Do not correct them -- they are the baseline the remaining campaign steps are measured against. A state='flipped' entry records a baseline that HAS been corrected and by which run. Full rationale: integration-tests/requirements_corpus/frozen_bugs.py

FV-1.flipped_by: S4 (one header-driven row parser -- column order stops being load-bearing, so a zero-row parse of a populated spec becomes impossible)
FV-1.now: The `zero-row-parse` fixture parses to one requirement (FR-06.01) and T1 FAILs on it. `absent` and `empty` still SKIP, correctly, and their evidence string is now distinguishable from the populated case. HONEST LIMIT: the TRIGGER is removed, not the guard. `if not requirements:` still cannot tell 'no spec' from 'spec I could not read', and S4 opened NEW routes to that state -- a spec whose ids are all non-canonical (`FR-7`, `FR-1.1`), or whose requirements table has no header naming a Priority column, now parses to zero rows. What makes those DIAGNOSABLE rather than silent is the `invalid_ids` accumulator: every declined row is recorded with a reason, published on the manifest, so a zero-row parse can be told apart from an empty repo by looking. CORRECTION (S4 second round, from external code review): this note previously routed the fix to S5's stated AC, which reads '`_column_map` distinguishes no-spec-on-disk from spec-present-no-recognised-header'. That does NOT cover the C1 route -- there the spec IS on disk and the header IS recognised; only the row ids fail. S5's AC has been amended to name the third state explicitly, and a triage anchor filed, so the safety net actually covers the hole it is pointed at.
FV-1.what: A spec that parses to ZERO rows makes traceability check T1 SKIP rather than FAIL.

FV-2.flipped_by: S6 (one catalog at one path: 'no requirements found' stops being a legitimate state)
FV-2.what: An empty requirement set reads GREEN across the audit plane -- 'no requirements' is treated as 'nothing to audit'.

FV-3.flipped_by: S4 (a header-driven reader selects by column NAME, so a row wider than its header can no longer shift the body)
FV-3.now: Both sites read the body from the column the header NAMES: `07-header-blind/FR-07.01` and `malformed/FR-01.03` now return 'ok'. The second site is worth noting -- the same bug had two occurrences in the corpus and only one was originally described.
FV-3.what: The RTM can display the WRONG requirement text.

FV-4.flipped_by: S4 (one header-driven reader, one accepted id column)
FV-4.now: A header row is recognised by naming a Priority column, not by its FIRST cell, so the id column may be headed `ID`, `FR`, or anything else. `05-fixture-fr` yields FR-05.01 instead of nothing.
FV-4.what: Group I silently audits NOTHING when a table header says 'FR' instead of 'ID'.

FV-5.now: The column map is set by a header ROW and replaced only by the next header row; headings do not reset it. group_i keeps FR-01.20. This is the rule that replaced BOTH extremes -- group_i's reset-at-every-heading and _requirement_parse's never-reset-at-all were the two ends of the same axis.
FV-5.what: Group I drops requirement rows that appear after any heading.

regenerated_for: S4 (iterate-2026-07-20-one-header-driven-parser): the five FR-table parsers collapse into one header-driven reader. FV-1, FV-3, FV-4 and FV-5 flip; FV-2 stays frozen for S6. The remaining movements are the convergence decisions the collapse forced -- one answer each for id strictness, column-map lifetime, invalid priorities, escaped pipes, row recognition, fold-map skipping, and (after external code review found a composition route that admitted false requirements) table governance: a row must sit under a header naming a Priority column and be wide enough to reach it. That last rule WITHDREW the headerless positional fallback and RE-DECIDED the minimum row width, which is why 'malformed'/FR-01.01 'ragged' is no longer a requirement in any parser -- it is recorded in the new invalid_ids accumulator instead. Every moved cell is attributed in ADR-107; absent/empty/greenfield/brownfield/spec-dir/planning-file are untouched.
